elections.note: Certified final = 1,239,405 ballots cast (CA SoS Statement of Vote, Voter Participation Statistics by County: Orange 'Total Voters' = Precinct 516,801 + VBM 722,604). FIRST post-poll-close (8:05 PM) report total NOT recoverable: OCROV's per-run 'Cumulative Results Report' PDF archive (fileadmin/live/.../Run_NN/cumulative.pdf) only begins with the Nov 2020 general; for 2016 only the final official results.htm survives, and the Wayback Machine is blocked in this environment. Pre-epollbook (OC adopted iPad e-pollbooks + Voter's Choice Act vote centers at the March 2020 primary). Proxy context only: OCROV's first 8:05 PM report is the VBM ballots received before Election Day; VBM returns stood at 384,675 as of Nov 3, 2016 (5 days out) and total VBM voters were 722,604, so the first tranche was large but its exact total is unpublished.

Certified final = 1,106,729 ballots cast (CA SoS Voter Participation Statistics by County: Orange 'Total Voters' = Precinct 393,423 + VBM 713,306). FIRST 8:05 PM report total NOT recoverable: OCROV per-run cumulative PDF archive starts in Nov 2020; for 2018 only the final results.htm survives, Wayback is blocked, and election-night news coverage (OC Register / Voice of OC / Patch) used live results widgets without static first-report figures. Pre-epollbook. Context: this was OC's highest-turnout midterm since 1970; counting ran 3.5 weeks (final released Fri Nov 23, 2018) and the large late VBM/provisional count broke Democratic, but the first-night ballot count itself was not published as a clean figure.

Election-night numerator = 1,028,800 ballots from OCROV's first post-poll-close report ('Cumulative Results Report' Run 1, Run Time 7:49 PM, Run Date 11/03/2020), with 0 of 1,795 precincts/vote-centers counted = all vote-by-mail received before Election Day. 1,028,800 / 1,546,570 certified = 66.52%. First general AFTER epollbook + Voter's Choice Act adoption (first used March 2020 primary). Denominator from CA SoS (Orange Total Voters 1,546,570 = Precinct 260,909 + VBM 1,285,661). The widely cited '~1 million ballots on election night' matches this primary figure.

Election-night numerator = 442,334 ballots from OCROV's first post-poll-close report (Cumulative Results Report Run 1, Run Time 8:00 PM, Run Date 11/08/2022), 0 of 2,169 precincts/vote-centers counted = all vote-by-mail received before Election Day. 442,334 / 994,277 certified = 44.49%. Post-epollbook. Denominator from CA SoS (Orange Total Voters 994,277 = Precinct 164,115 + VBM 830,162).

Election-night numerator = 718,019 ballots from OCROV's first post-poll-close report (Cumulative Results Report Run 1, Run Time 8:00 PM, Run Date 11/05/2024), 0 of 2,294 precincts/vote-centers counted = all vote-by-mail received before Election Day. 718,019 / 1,417,397 certified = 50.66%. Post-epollbook. Denominator from CA SoS (Orange Total Voters 1,417,397 = Precinct 323,223 + VBM 1,094,174). ASV never adopted (OC verifies signatures by hand).

notes: Definition: election-night % = ballots in OCROV's FIRST post-poll-close 'Cumulative Results Report' (Run 1, ~8:00 PM, all vote-by-mail / 0 precincts counted) divided by CA SoS certified final ballots cast. Adoption: Orange County deployed iPad electronic pollbooks and Voter's Choice Act vote centers starting the March 3, 2020 presidential primary, so the Nov 2020 general is the first POST-epollbook general; 2016 and 2018 are PRE. Automated signature verification (ASV) was NEVER adopted (staff compare envelope-image signatures by hand), so vs_asv = 'n/a' (no adoption boundary) for every election. Primary numerators recovered directly from OCROV Run_01 cumulative.pdf reports (archive exists for 2020/2022/2024 at fileadmin/live/<GEN2020|Gen2022|GEN2024>/Run_01/cumulative.pdf). Pre-adoption first-report totals (2016, 2018) are unavailable: OCROV's per-run PDF archive does not predate Nov 2020, only final results.htm pages survive, and the Wayback Machine is blocked in this environment. Like-to-like takeaways: PRESIDENTIAL first-report share fell from a COVID-era high of 66.5% (2020, post) to 50.7% (2024, post) -- no clean pre-epollbook presidential anchor (2016) is sourceable; MIDTERM post-adoption first-report share was 44.5% (2022) with no sourceable pre anchor (2018). The e-pollbook adoption thus cannot be cleanly tested against a pre-adoption first-report number here because OC did not publish/archive a retrievable first-report total before the 2020 general.
